Notice Of Motion And Motion To Set Aside Default Of Defendant Martin Eng
Off Calendar Sep-10-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT MARTIN ENG's Motion To Set Aside Default Of Defendant Martin Eng:

Defendant Martin Eng's motion to set aside the default entered against him on September 11, 2019 is denied as untimely. A motion to set aside a default brought under Code of Civil Procedure Section 473(b) must be filed within six months. (Code Civ. Proc. 473(b).) "The six-month limit is mandatory; a court has no authority to grant relief under section 473, subdivision (b), unless an application is made within the six-month period." (Arumbula v. Union Carbide Corp. (2005) 128 Cal.App.4th 333, 340.) The Court has no authority to grant relief from the six-month limit, whether based on mistake, inadvertence, surprise, or excusable neglect or an attorney's affidavit of fault. (Id. at 345; accord, Austin v. Los Angeles Unified School Dist. (2016) 244 Cal.App.4th 918, 918 ["The six-month time limitation is jurisdictional; the court has no power to grant relief under section 473 once the time has lapsed."].) Defendant's alternative motion under Code of Civil Procedure section 473.5 is also untimely. Under that statute, a motion to set aside a default or default judgment may be granted if the moving party establishes that service has not resulted in actual notice in time to defend the action, if the motion is filed within a reasonable time, but in no event more than 180 days of service of notice of default. (Code Civ. Proc. 473.5.) Here, the docket establishes that notice of entry of the default was served on Defendant by mail on September 13, 2019, and Defendant does not meet his burden to show that he did not have actual notice of the pendency of the action in time to defend it. To the contrary, the record establishes that Defendant had extensive telephonic and email communications with Plaintiffs' counsel regarding the action between June and December 2018, and again in 2019. (O'Neal Decl. 2-4 & Ex. 1; Stroman Decl. 2-3, Exs. 1, 2.)

Counsel for the plaintiffs is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the adopted ruling and in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: S. Le, Not Reported. =(302/EPS)
